channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This tiny Ryzen 9 PC can drive three, yes three, 8K monitors thanks to a low profile video card with a proper Geforce RTX 4060 GPU - but the best part is that it costs less than $1000 with 32GB RAM and 1TB SSD
item.description: Minisforum 790S7 is compact, powerful, and won’t take up too much desk space alongside several monitors.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Why you shouldn’t store your money in payment apps
item.description: As use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Good customer experience isn’t just good. For federal agencies, it’s the law
item.description: "The purpose of the act is to improve government websites and its related services to enhance that customer experience," said GAO's Carol Harris. The post Good customer experience isn’t just good. For federal agencies, it’s the law first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: SpaceX sues California regulators over rejected launches, claiming political bias
item.description: SpaceX filed a lawsuit against a California commission Tuesday, accusing members of political bias after they rejected the aerospace company’s request to allow more rocket launches from an air base in the state.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AI isn’t really that smart yet, Apple researchers warn
item.description: While we wait for the Age Of Apple Intelligence, it may be worth considering a recent Apple research study that exposes critical weaknesses in existing artificial intelligence models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission is taking steps to make it easier for consumers to cancel digital subscriptions.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Making technology a company capability at Foot Locker
item.description: Retail is dynamic, ever-changing, and generates a lot of data, and through merchandising, in-store transactions, supply chain, digital, and pricing, the opportunities to leverage data are endless.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cómo gestionar los programas de IA generativa: gobernanza, educación, regulación
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa ha crecido con gran rapidez, atrayendo inversiones y despertando el interés y la creatividad de los desarrolladores. Las empresas ven en la IA generativa una vía para crear servicios innovadores y revolucionar sus sectores.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI can predict extreme weather, but here’s where the forecasts fall short
item.description: When floods ripped through parts of Europe in September, the scale of the destruction took people by surprise. The intense rains should not have, because those had been predicted by sophisticated forecasting systems enhanced with artificial intelligence. But forewarned did not mean forearmed.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SpaceX catches Starship rocket booster on Texas launchpad
item.description: SpaceX caught a rocket booster on its Texas launchpad as it returned to Earth on Sunday. The chopstick-like arms of the tower, called Mechazilla, grabbed the descending 232-foot booster after a seven-minute flight.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft
item.description: The Army Corps of Engineers will complete its hybrid cloud implementation by the end of fiscal 2025. Dovarius Peoples, former Corps’ CIO, lays out where the org The post Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available as a beta release. With the update, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5
